title: thymus membranaceus var. murcicus
intro: Thymus membranaceus var. murcicus, also known as Spanish thyme, is a variant of the thyme plant species. It is a low-growing, perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, known for its fragrance and medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of Spanish thyme have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions, thanks to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Spanish thyme can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or added to food and herbal remedies to promote relaxation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system.
